summary: Problem 7 asks whether a uniform lattice with 2-torsion in a real semi-simple group can be the fundamental group of a closed manifold whose universal cover is ℚ-acyclic. The answer is 'No', supported by a clean Euler-characteristic obstruction: an order-2 element acts freely on the ℚ-acyclic universal cover ~M (which has Euler characteristic 1), but a free ℤ/2 action forces the Euler characteristic to be even. No actual proof artifact currently exists to evaluate — the tracked issue was a seed/placeholder and was closed without a submitted proof.
problems: No concrete proof has been written or submitted; the only issue was a placeholder seed task that was closed with 'no proof to evaluate'.
Best-proof record shows '100 open issues' and an unspecified experiment, indicating the verification/evaluation pipeline has not engaged with any real candidate proof.

The sketched obstruction must rigorously justify that the order-2 element acts FREELY as a deck transformation — this requires that Γ act freely on ~M (true for a manifold universal cover) AND that the torsion element be nontrivial in Γ, which needs explicit statement.

The Euler characteristic argument needs care over ℚ: ~M is ℚ-acyclic but generally non-compact/infinite, so χ(~M)=1 and the free-action parity argument must be applied to the compact quotient M (χ(M) via transfer) rather than to ~M directly.

The role of the semi-simple/lattice hypothesis (e.g., cohomological dimension, virtual duality, Borel-conjecture-type rigidity) is not yet made explicit in the obstruction; it is unclear whether it is essential or merely contextual.
highlights: A short, elegant 'No' answer is identified with a clear mechanism: free ℤ/2 action on a ℚ-acyclic space and Euler-characteristic parity.
Solvability score of 58% reflects a genuinely tractable problem with a known obstruction strategy.
The placeholder issue has already been correctly triaged and resolved as a non-substantive tracker, so there is no contaminating false-positive proof in the record.
The problem is well-scoped with an authoritative source (Weinberger) and a crisp yes/no formulation, making a full write-up feasible.

suggested_todos.title: Write a complete proof of the 'No' answer: formalize free ℤ/2 deck action on ~M and derive contradiction via Euler-characteristic / transfer parity on the compact quotient M
Make rigorous the transition from χ(~M)=1 (ℚ-acyclic) to an even-χ constraint on M, citing the transfer homomorphism / Smith theory for free finite group actions
State and justify whether the semi-simple-lattice hypothesis is needed, or note that the argument applies to any group with 2-torsion as π1 of such a manifold
Open a new substantive proof issue to replace the closed placeholder seed task, and route the drafted proof through the verification pipeline
Add references (Smith theory, transfer, Brown's group cohomology Euler characteristic) and edge-case discussion of orientability and ℚ vs integral coefficients
